findings.evidence: `rg -n "isJHolomorphic(On|WithinAt|At)?_(const|id)|IsJHolomorphic\.isJHolomorphicOn" code/TauCeti/Geometry/Symplectic/JHolomorphic.lean` shows const/id lemmas at lines 198, 204, 219, 226, 266, and 272, but no `isJHolomorphicOn_const` or `isJHolomorphicOn_id` for the setwise predicate defined at lines 55-57.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp] lemma isJHolomorphicOn_const ... : IsJHolomorphicOn J J' (fun _ : V => c) s` and `@[simp] lemma isJHolomorphicOn_id ... : IsJHolomorphicOn J J id s`, proved via the within-at lemmas or `IsJHolomorphic.isJHolomorphicOn`.
findings.issue: `IsJHolomorphicOn` is a main public predicate but has no `@[simp]` constant or identity lemmas, unlike `IsJHolomorphicAt`, `IsJHolomorphicWithinAt`, and `IsJHolomorphic`.

summary: The pointwise/within/global predicates now have characteristic restatements, but the setwise public predicate is missing the same normal-form API for constants and identity maps. Users of `IsJHolomorphicOn` must route through global lemmas or unfold the definition instead of using the exposed setwise surface directly.
